title: Cloning the Self for Mental Well-Being: A Framework for Designing Safe and Therapeutic Self-Clone Chatbots
one_sentence_summary: This study develops a design framework for therapeutic self-clone chatbots by analyzing the affordances, risks, and ethical implications identified through interviews with mental health experts and users.
research_problem: While self-clone chatbots offer novel opportunities for intra-personal exploration, they present significant risks regarding identity confusion, negative reinforcement, and blurred user agency that require responsible design guidance.
method_summary: The researchers conducted in-depth interviews with 16 mental health experts (providers and researchers) and 6 prospective end-users to identify tensions and design opportunities.
dataset_or_materials: Qualitative interview data from 16 mental health professionals and 6 general users.
main_findings: The authors produced a design framework organized around defining therapeutic goals, establishing design dimensions, and implementing safety guardrails to guide the development of safe self-clone systems.

section_map.heading: Cloning the Self for Mental Well-Being: A Framework for Designing Safe and Therapeutic Self-Clone Chatbots
